title: onobrychis tournefortii subsp. cappadocica
intro: Onobrychis tournefortii subsp. cappadocica, also known as Cappadocian sainfoin or Cappadocian bird's-foot trefoil, is a plant species belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to the region of Cappadocia in Turkey. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Onobrychis tournefortii subsp. cappadocica include reducing inflammation, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. The plant is prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
